compiled_state.description:
# Batch-commit write primitive for bulk operations (import/migration scale)

## Context

Discovered from the export/import design brainstorm (`rule-swamp-pupil`, 2026-06-17). rebar's write path commits ONE git commit per event under a per-event lock acquire/release (`src/rebar/_store/event_append.py:82-146`, `src/rebar/_store/lock.py:187-231`); no batch/bulk/transaction primitive exists. A bulk import of N tickets composes N×~3 events → thousands of commits + thousands of lock cycles (~3.3 min of lock overhead alone for ~2,000 events, before commit/push). `rule-swamp-pupil` already mitigates network amplification (`REBAR_SYNC_PUSH=off` + single final push), but the per-event commit/lock cost remains. This task is the real fix, deferred until real-world store sizes demand it.

## What / Implementation detail

Add `_store.batch_stage_and_commit(events)`: under ONE write lock, write/rename M event files, one `git add`, ONE commit. Crucially, **each event remains its own file** (unique name) — so cross-clone union-merge and SNAPSHOT compaction (which key off event UUIDs, not commit boundaries) are unaffected, and invariants I5 (single locked writer) and I2 (composed events) hold. Route the importer (and optionally the reconciler applier) through it in chunks (per-ticket or per-N). Atomicity: a single commit of M staged files is atomic at the git level; partial staging before commit is recoverable because import is idempotent (resume by `source_id`).

## Success Criteria

- A bulk import of a large synthetic store completes in a fraction of the per-event wall-clock, with the same resulting logical state.
- Cross-clone convergence + compaction unaffected (regression tests).
- The single-event write path is unchanged.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `_store.batch_stage_and_commit(events)`: one lock, M event files, one commit; each event its own file
- [ ] importer routes events through it in chunks; single-event path unchanged
- [ ] atomicity + crash-resume (idempotent) tests; union-merge + compaction regression tests
- [ ] benchmark: large-import wall-clock before vs after

Deferred optimization for `rule-swamp-pupil`; build if store sizes demand it.
compiled_state.title: Batch-commit write primitive for bulk operations (import/migration scale)
